observations.recommendation:
a. Cause the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) by the Property Officer/Unit to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties; and
b. Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386, to prevent further deterioration and to realize income therefrom if found to be saleable.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period;
b. Exhaust efforts to obtain the records or transaction documents of Due from NGAs, Due from LGUs and Other Receivables in order to confirm the existence and validity of the receivables; and
c. In case of non-retrieval of the documents, disclose the amounts and their breakdowns in the Notes to Financial Statements and prepare aging of dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers on a quarterly basis (Annexes 1-3, COA Circular 2023-008) and prepare a request for write-off of dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts and prepare necessary adjusting entry to correct affected accounts in the financial statements and furnish COA the corrections made therein.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving prior year’s financial transaction documents

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Exhaust efforts to trace the details of Due to NGAs prior years’ balances and prepare separate subsidiary ledgers as required under Item 6 of COA Circular No. 2004-008 and Section 10, Volume II of the NGAS Manual; 
b. Henceforth, all fund transfer from NGAs should be covered by MOA and to be recorded as provided in COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and to properly monitor the receipts and utilization of fund transfers from NGAs; and
c. Return immediately any unutilized balances to the source agency.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving prior year's financial transaction documents

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Account to revert the unexpended LDRRMF
balances in the Trust Liabilities – DRRM account which have exceeded
the five-year validity period amounting ₱1.872 million back to the
General Fund by preparing a JEV using the prescribed correcting entry:
Dr. Trust Liabilities – DRRM 1,871,607.46
Cr. Prior Years’ Adjustments 1,871,607.46
b. Strengthen monitoring of LDRRMF utilization to ensure that
unexpended balances are accurately recorded in the Trust Liability –
DRRM account and promptly reverted to the General Fund once the
five-year validity period has lapsed;
c. Direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council
(DRRMC) to identify the projects and activities to be charged out of the
unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF and submit a revised AIP duly
approved by the Local Sanggunian and the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
upon recommendation of the LDRRMC in accordance with the
approved LDRRMP; and
d. Ensure that the LDRRMFIP includes the unexpended previous years’
LDRRMF under separate caption and to include the list of projects and
activities to be undertaken.

2.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to require:
a. The Municipal Accountant to:
1. Record purchases of supplies and materials for stock to their
appropriate inventory accounts and recognize as expense only when
stocks are issued upon receipt of the SSMI with duly validated original
copy of the RIS pursuant to Section 121 of the manual on the NGAS for
LGUs Vol. 1; and
2. Maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records including the
Supplies Ledger Cards for each commodity/stock in accordance with
Section 114 of the manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
b. The Storekeeper to:
1. Consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) for which
supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit it immediately, along with the
RIS, to the OIC-Municipal Accountant as basis of JEV to recognize
expenses based on the cost of the supplies and materials issued; and
2. Conduct a semestral physical count of inventory items by type,
prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), and
submit the results to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and
January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters,
respectively.

3.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to require:
a. Cause the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report for
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) by the Property Officer/Unit to
facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties; and
b. Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of unserviceable
properties following the procedures set forth in Section 2.1 and 2.2 of
COA and DBM Joint Circular, to prevent further deterioration and to
realize income therefrom if found to be saleable.

4.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Exert all efforts to trace and reconstruct the details of prior years’
balances under the “Due to NGAs” account, and establish separate and
accurate subsidiary ledgers for each transaction to ensure proper
monitoring and reconciliation; and
b. Promptly return any unutilized or excess fund balances to their
respective Source Agencies in accordance with the terms and
conditions stipulated in the MOAs or relevant agreements.

5.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Maintain a repair and maintenance record/history of each equipment or
vehicle indicating the type of repair undertaken and the date when it was
made to avoid unnecessary repair and maintenance expenses; and
b. Attach repair and maintenance history/record to Disbursement Vouchers
as supporting document.

6.12 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Council (MPDC), in coordination with the
MEO, to closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the identified
and prioritized development PPAs embodied in the LDP and AIP.

7.8 We recommend that Management adopt the payroll-through-bank system
provided by the AGDB to streamline salary disbursements, minimize risks,
and promote transparency, accountability, and efficiency in financial
transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the Committee to conduct inventory on the 16 PPE accounts and prepare/submit an updated Report on the Physical Count of PPEs; and
b. Direct the Property Officer to maintain a Property Card (PC) for each class of PPE, update the PCs based on the RPCPPE and reconcile with accounting records to ensure the correct balances of PPE accounts; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated PPELC for each class of PPE and reconcile with the updated RPCPPE and PC maintained by Property Unit/Office. Henceforth, make the necessary adjustments on any discrepancies.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to re-calculate the amount of depreciation expense in order to adjust the carrying amount of Road Networks and account for the change in accounting estimate in accordance with IPSAS 3 - Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors.
We recommended and Management agreed to terminate and utilize these funds for projects that could immediately benefit the constituents.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the provisions of Section 59 of MNGAS, Volume I and Section 97 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and make the necessary adjusting entries as follows:

a. For checks which remained outstanding after six months but within two years from date of issue:

Account Title Dr. Cr.
Cash in Bank, LCCA xx
Accounts Payable (or appropriate Liability account previously debited) xx

To record the receipt of the original lost/spoiled/staled Check No. ____dated ____ issued in favor of ______ (notice of stoppage of payment issued to the bank) and request for replacement.

b. For checks which remained outstanding after two years from date of issue:

Account Title
Dr. Cash in Bank, LCCA xx
Cr. Government Equity xx

To record the receipt of the original lost/spoiled/staled Check No. ____dated ____ issued in favor of ______ (notice of stoppage of payment issued to the bank).

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Demand the Accountable Officer and other persons concerned to liquidate their outstanding cash advances as required under COA Circular No. 97-002 and refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to use the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer in recording the grant of cash advances for special purpose/time-bound undertakings so as to properly recognize accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Discontinue charges of personal services and administrative expenses to the 20% Development Fund;
b. Ensure that all charges and expenditures in the 20% DF comply with the allowable expenses pursuant to the guidelines set forth under the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020; and
c. Require the Municipal Budget Office to review/evaluate carefully the expenditure items included in the proposed programs/projects budgeted under the 20% Development Fund, to prevent the disbursement of disallowable items as stated under Item 3.2.5 of the JMC No. 2020-01.

We recommend that management demand the Accountable Officer and other persons concerned to liquidate their outstanding cash advances as required under COA Circular No. 97-002 and refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with unsettled accounts.

We recommend that management:

Direct the Budget Officer to allocate fund/budget for the survey/relocation and titling of untitled lots; and 

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to initiate and supervise the proper proceedings for the land titling and to process the documents necessary in securing Certificate of Title of the untitled lots to ensure absolute ownership and to protect the legal interest of the LGU, pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular 92-386.

1.12 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct inventory on the 16 PPE
accounts and prepare/submit an updated Report on the Physical Count of
PPEs;
b. Direct the Property Officer to maintain a Property Card (PC) for each
class of PPE, update the PCs based on the RPCPPE and reconcile with
accounting records to ensure the correct balances of PPE accounts; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated PPELC for
each class of PPE and reconcile with the updated RPCPPE and PC
maintained by Property Unit/Office. Henceforth, make the necessary
adjustments on any discrepancies.

2.6 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant
to re-calculate the amount of depreciation expense in order to adjust the
carrying amount of Road Networks and account for the change in accounting
estimate in accordance with IPSAS 3 - Accounting Policies, Changes in
Accounting Estimates and Errors.

3.7 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Cause the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report for
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) by the Property Officer/Unit to facilitate
the disposal of unserviceable properties; and
b. Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of unserviceable
properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386,
to prevent further deterioration and to realize income therefrom if found
to be saleable.

4.6 We recommended and Management agreed to terminate and utilize these funds
for projects that could immediately benefit the constituents.

5.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
strictly comply with the provisions of Section 59 of MNGAS, Volume I and
Section 97 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and make the necessary
adjusting entries as follows:
a. For checks which remained outstanding after six months but within two
years from date of issue:
Account Title Dr. Cr.
Cash in Bank, LCCA xx
Accounts Payable (or appropriate
Liability account previously debited) xx
To record the receipt of the original lost/spoiled/staled Check No. ____dated ____
issued in favor of ______ (notice of stoppage of payment issued to the bank) and
request for replacement.
b. For checks which remained outstanding after two years from date of issue:
Account Title Dr. Cr.
Cash in Bank, LCCA xx
Government Equity xx
c.
To record the receipt of the original lost/spoiled/staled Check No. ____dated ____
issued in favor of ______ (notice of stoppage of payment issued to the bank).

6.8 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Demand the Accountable Officer and other persons concerned to liquidate
their outstanding cash advances as required under COA Circular No. 97-
002 and refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and
employees with unsettled accounts; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to use the account Advances to Special
Disbursing Officer in recording the grant of cash advances for special
purpose/time-bound undertakings so as to properly recognize accounts in
the financial statements.

7.6 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the
existence of the receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when
these become due and demandable and that fund transfers are liquidated
within the prescribed period;
b. Exhaust efforts to obtain the records or transaction documents of Due
from NGAs, Due from LGUs and Other Receivables in order to confirm
the existence and validity of the receivables; and
c. In case of non-retrieval of the documents, disclose the amounts and their
breakdowns in the Notes to Financial Statements and prepare aging of
dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers on a
quarterly basis (Annexes 1-3, COA Circular 2023-008) and prepare a
request for write-off of dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

8.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts and prepare necessary
adjusting entry to correct affected accounts in the financial statements and
furnish COA the corrections made therein.

9.9 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Exhaust efforts to trace the details of Due to NGAs prior years’ balances
and prepare separate subsidiary ledgers;
b. Henceforth, all fund transfer from NGAs should be covered by MOA and
to be recorded as provided in COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December
13, 1994 and to properly monitor the receipts and utilization of fund
transfers from NGAs; and
c. Return immediately any unutilized balances to the source agency.

10.13 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Discontinue charges of personal services and administrative expenses to
the 20% Development Fund;
b. Ensure that all charges and expenditures in the 20% DF comply with the
allowable expenses pursuant to the guidelines set forth under the DBM-
DOF-DILG JMC 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020; and
c. Require the Municipal Budget Office to review/evaluate carefully the
expenditure items included in the proposed programs/projects budgeted
under the 20% Development Fund, to prevent the disbursement of
disallowable items as stated under Item 3.2.5 of the JMC No. 2020-01.

11.13 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Ensure the timely utilization of the LDRRMF in accordance with
the approved LDRRMP and that the STF is utilized in accordance
with Item 6.4 and 6.5 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-
1;
b. Direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Council (DRRMC) to identify the projects and activities to be
charged out of the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF and
submit a revised AIP duly approved by the Local Sanggunian and
the Local Chief Executive (LCE) upon recommendation of the
LDRRMC in accordance with the approved LDRRMP;
c. Ensure that the LDRRMP includes the unexpended previous years’
LDRRMF;
d. Direct the LDRRMC to identify and prioritize PPAs that are
feasible, doable and can be completed within the target date of
implementation taking into consideration the thrust set by the LGU
leadership;
e. Direct all concerned officials to optimize the utilization of
LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities that will
strengthen its capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks;
f. Facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of
fund and assess its implementation to immediately address any
arising concerns/problems that can be mitigated.

We recommend that management:

Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 77-61 and ensure that all disbursements for fuel consumption (gasoline and oil) of government vehicles are duly supported by properly accomplished and approved serially Driver’s Trip Tickets (Annex K-2); and

Require the concerned drivers to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels (see Annex K-3) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor; and 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to book up as fuel, oil and lubricants expense those that are duly supported by the Monthly Report of Official Travels, Report of Fuel Consumption, with a duly filled up Driver’s Trip Tickets.

We recommend that management:

Require the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) and Budget Committee to ensure that all identified and prioritized development PPAs funded by 20% DF are investment and capital expenditures in nature, specific, well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to expedite execution of these to guarantee the prompt accomplishment of the same;

Require the MPDC, in coordination with the MEO, to closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized development PPAs embodied in the LDP and AIP; and

Direct Budget Officer to reassess unimplemented projects that are of the nature of MOOE and projects with unused balance in the 20% Development Fund.

We recommend that management ensure the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that management:

Direct the Municipal Health Officer and/or concerned RHU personnel to prepare and maintain a Summary/List of donated vaccines received, distributed and balances based on the updated stock cards and furnish the Municipal Accountant, of record of the receipt and distribution of vaccines, ancillaries and Supplies Inventory Report or its equivalent in relation to the vaccination program; and 

Require the Municipal Accountant to adapt the perpetual inventory system and record the vaccines received and its distribution as reported by the LGU to DOH-CHD, and draw Journal Entry Voucher for the applicable inventory accounts to record the remaining supplies of vaccines in stock at Dec. 31, 2022.

Require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAs) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. Likewise, assistance by other offices be extended to Supply Officer to audit inventory.
 
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any; and

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Create an  Inventory Committee and come up with a Physical Inventory Plan  to facilitate the  submission of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
b.  Cause the reconciliation of the  property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any; and
c.  Require  the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  and  the Store Keeper to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of PPE, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Process  the  documents  necessary  in securing  Certificate of Title of the untitled lots to ensure absolute ownership and to protect the legal interest of the LGU, pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular 92-386; and
Direct  the  Budget  Officer  to  allocate  fund  intended   for   the survey/ relocation and titling of these lots, and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to initiate and supervise the proper proceedings for the land titling and to cause the immediate transfer to the Municipality to ascertain the ownership.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Direct the Local  Chief  Executive  to  issue/cause the renewal of the contract of lease in CY 2021 to qualified lessees, and include in the terms and conditions, stipulations for the lessees compliance of its duties and responsibilities. Enforce compliance to the terms of contract of lease executed between the LGU and the qualified new lessees before they are authorized to occupy the stall to protect the interest of the LGU.
Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor collection of monthly stall rentals, as well as for the overdue amounts, impose surcharges from delinquent market stall holders and the penalties set forth under the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality;
Direct  the  Acting Municipal Treasurer to  prepare Status Report of  Rental to include information on stall numbers existing occupants of rate/month, period of last payment and the total amount due; and
Strictly  comply  with  the  pertinent  Sections  of  the  2016  Revised  Revenue Code of the Municipality.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Direct  the  Local  Chief  Executive  to cause  the  concerned  department heads to properly execute the full implementation of the 20% development programs and projects as planned to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated; and
Strict compliance with Section 287 of RA No. 7160 and Section 5 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended and the management agreed for the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply and submit within the prescribed period Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements as provided in Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation       statements and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor regularly within the period prescribed under Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445; and

Request  the  depository  banks  to furnish them bank statements/snapshots immediately every end of the month for prompt preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements.
We recommended and the Management agreed to cause the immediate implementation of identified priority programs/projects/activities funded from the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the  implementation and regular monitoring of GAD related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided and  see to it that PPAs are  in  conformity  with  Section1.2  of Joint  Circular  No. 2004-1 of  DBM, NEDA and  NCRFW and Section  4.1 (c.1.4)  of  Joint Memorandum  Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Designate to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of MDRRMF for the year ending December 31, 2020.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to RA 10121 otherwise known “the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.

Improve its collection effort by regularly and properly sending out notices to delinquent taxpayers; and 

Enforce collection remedies provided under Sections 256 and 260 of RA No. 7160, particularly the sale of delinquent properties through public auction in order to demonstrate the municipality’s will power and capability to enforce the laws on real property taxation and deter tax evasions.
observations.managementAction: The management was not able to send notices to delinquent taxpayers due to COVID19 pandemic. The Acting Municipal Treasurer assured that they will start sending notices to delinquent taxpayers after the Real Property Tax Amnesty imposed by the Province of Capiz.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the issuance and renewal of the contract of lease in CY 2020 for qualified lessees and include stipulations therein for the compliance of the existing duties and responsibilities. See to it that contract of lease is executed between the LGU and the qualified new lessees before they are authorized to occupy the stall.

Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to intensify efforts in the collection of monthly stall rentals as well as the overdue amounts including surcharges from delinquent market stall holders and strictly impose the penalties set forth under the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management will resume the issuance of the contract of lease and renewal of contracts to qualified lessees and will send notices/reminderfor the stall owners with delinquent stall rentals.

observations.recommendation:
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and   effect necessary adjustments, if any.

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
a.  Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly bank reconciliation       statements and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor regularly within the period prescribed under Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445; and
b.  Request  the  depository  banks  to furnish them bank statements/snapshots immediately every end of the month for prompt preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAs) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. Likewise, assistance by other offices be extended to Supply Officer to audit inventory.
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any; and
Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
We recommended and the Management agreed to cause the immediate implementation of identified priority programs/projects/activities funded from the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the  implementation and regular monitoring of GAD related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided and  see to it that PPAs are  in  conformity  with  Section1.2  of Joint  Circular  No. 2004-1 of  DBM, NEDA and  NCRFW and Section  4.1 (c.1.4)  of  Joint Memorandum  Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Designate to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of MDRRMF for the year ending December 31, 2020.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to RA 10121 otherwise known “the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
a.  Instruct the Municaipal Accountant to immediately submit the aforementioned   supporting documents to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the Bayanihan to Grants to Cities and Municipalities (BGCM) transactions amounting to ₱7.124 million;
b.  Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to thoroughly verify each disbursement as to the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents to strengthen internal control in the disbursement process; and
c.   Adhere to the procedures and requirements provided in Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445, Item 9.2.5 of COA  Circular No. 2012-001  dated  June 14, 2012  and  GPPB  Circular  No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 particularly in the conduct of emergency procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible and furnish the certified list to the OIC-Municipal Accountant as her basis in taking up the RPT/SET receivables and deferred income in order for the financial statements to be fairly presented and strictly comply with  Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAs for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer has started preparing the certified list of tax payers and the amount collectible.

observations.recommendation:
Exert effort in the processing of Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the LGU pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular 92-386

Set aside fund for the titling of these lots and direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper payments of expenses for the land titling and to update and reconcile the inventory of property assets with the updated records of the OIC-Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will designate personnel who will take charge in the Titling of several lots owned by the LGU of Sapian, hence this was not among the duties and responsibilities of the Municipal Assessor. Likewise, the Supplemental Budget is now being prepared for the titling expenses.

observations.recommendation:
Reclassify completed projects totaling ₱14.422 million included in the Construction in Progress account and transfer to the appropriate asset  account as follows:

Recommended entry upon completion of the project:

For Construction in Progress – Infrastructure Assets

Debit (appropriate Infra. Asset Account)  -                               ₱12,672,477.54

Credit  Const. in progress -  Infrastructure Asset  –                 ₱12,672,477.54

For Construction in Progress – Buildings and Other Structures

Debit (appropriate Buildings and Other Structures Account) – ₱1,749,572.00
       
Credit  Const. in progress – Bldgs.  and  Other  Structures   – ₱1,749,572.00                   
           
Prepare journal entries for the accumulated depreciation and depreciation expenses; and

Henceforth, comply strictly to Section 50 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Vol. I, and COA Circular  No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

Stop utilizing the 20% DF for the construction, rehabilitation/repair and maintenance  of  several  buildings  and  other  structures  and  infra.  assets  of various public schools instead of other priority socio-economic development projects and programs to ensure compliance with the provision of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. 

Funding of programs, projects and activities of public schools should be charged against SEF.

We recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.

Additional regular personnel will be assigned in the Municipal Accountant’

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:

a. Improve its collection effort by regularly and properly sending out notices to
delinquent taxpayers; and

b. Enforce collection remedies provided under Sections 256 and 260 of RA No.
7160, particularly the sale of delinquent properties through public auction in order
to demonstrate the municipality’s will power and capability to enforce the laws on
real property taxation and deter tax evasions.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:

a. Direct the issuance and renewal of the contract of lease in CY 2020 for qualified
lessees and include stipulations therein for the compliance of the existing duties
and responsibilities. See to it that contract of lease is executed between the LGU
and the qualified new lessees before they are authorized to occupy the stall.

b. Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to intensify efforts in the collection of
monthly stall rentals as well as the overdue amounts including surcharges from
delinquent market stall holders and strictly impose the penalties set forth under
the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management require the Acting
Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of taxpayers and the amount
due and collectible and furnish the certified list to the OIC-Municipal Accountant
as her basis in taking up the RPT/SET receivables and deferred income in order for
the financial statements to be fairly presented and strictly comply with Section 20 of
the Manual on the NGAs for LGUs, Volume I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:

a. Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and
effect necessary adjustments, if any.

b. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to
immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property,
plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS
for LGUs, Volume I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:

a. Exert effort in the processing of Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in
order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the
LGU pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular 92-386
b. Set aside fund for the titling of these lots and direct the Acting Municipal
Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper payments of expenses for
the land titling and to update and reconcile the inventory of property
assets with the updated records of the OIC-Municipal Accountant.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:

a. Reclassify completed projects totaling ₱14.422 million included in the
Construction in Progress account and transfer to the appropriate asset account
as follows:
Recommended entry upon completion of the project:
For Construction in Progress – Infrastructure Assets
Debit (appropriate Infra. Asset Account) - ₱12,672,477.54
Credit Const. in progress - Infrastructure Asset – ₱12,672,477.54
For Construction in Progress – Buildings and Other Structures
Debit (appropriate Buildings and Other Structures Account) – ₱1,749,572.00
Credit Const. in progress – Bldgs. and Other Structures – ₱1,749,572.00

b. Prepare journal entries for the accumulated depreciation and depreciation
expenses; and

c. Henceforth, comply strictly to Section 50 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Vol.
I, and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that management:

a. Stop utilizing the 20% DF for the construction, rehabilitation/repair and
maintenance of several buildings and other structures and infra. assets of
various public schools instead of other priority socio-economic development
projects and programs to ensure compliance with the provision of DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

b. Funding of programs, projects and activities of public schools should be charged
against SEF.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the immediate submission of the Reports
of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents,
Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits
and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to
it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No.
95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.

It is also recommended that additional regular personnel will be assigned in the
Municipal Accountant’s Office to avoid delays in the submission of accounts.

We recommended that the Management fully utilized the 20% DF allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development as provided under Section 287 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 5 of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
Management should consider feasibility and monitoring of implementation of PPAs funded under the 20% DF.

We recommended that Management require the immediate allocation of fund pertaining to the construction of MRF for the five (5) barangays and construct the said projects immediately so as to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives thereof in compliance with Section 32 of R.A. No. 9003.

a. Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided and see to it that PPAs are in conformity with Section1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA, so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.
b. Conduct proper and regular monitoring of the implementation of GAD programs and projects so that full implementation of the same will be achieved.

We recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.
It is also recommended that additional regular personnel will be assigned in the Municipal Accountant’s Office to avoid delays in the submission of accounts.

We recommended that management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to see to it that the monthly net take home pay of an employee should not be lower than ₱5,000.00 before issuing certification to lending institutions in strict compliance to Section 48 of Republic Act. No. 10924 also known as General Appropriation Act of 2018.

a. Require an official/ employee who intends to be separated from the service due to reassignment/ transfer/retirement to refund unliquidated cash advances before issuance of clearance from accountabilities

We recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.

a. Ensure that lined programs, projects and activities under the 70% allocation for LDRRMF will directly address disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness issues in the locality and that the same be fully implemented during the year; and
b. Strictly comply with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012 on the utilization of LDRRM and relevant provisions of RA No. 10121

We recommended that Management appropriate funds for MRF and immediately utilize the same for full implementation of the project so as to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives thereof in compliance with Section 32 of R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that Management fully implement priority PPAs of GAD and see to it that PPAs are in conformity with Section 1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA, so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to Management the following:
Require immediate liquidation of cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has  been served and refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been settled/liquidated in strict compliance with Section  89 P.D. 1445 and Section  5.1.3  of  COA Circular No. 97-9002 dated February 10, 1997;
b.  Send final demand letter to persons who have retired or out of service who still have unliquidated cash advances; and
c. Comply strictly with the guidelines on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 and require the    OIC-Municipal Accountant to monitor compliance thereto to prevent the accumulation of cash advances.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
Require the Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.
Require the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management fully utilized the       20% DF allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development as provided under Section 287 of  R.A. No. 7160 and Section 5 of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
It is also recommended that during planning phase management should consider feasibility and monitoring of implementation of PPAs funded under the 20% DF.

In view thereof, we recommended that Management require the immediate allocation of fund pertaining to the construction of MRF for the five (5) barangays and construct the said projects immediately  so as to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives thereof in compliance with Section 32 of R.A. No. 9003.

In view of the foregoing, it is recommended that  Management:
Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided and  see to it that PPAs are in conformity with Section1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.
Conduct proper and regular monitoring of the implementation of GAD programs and projects so that full implementation of the same will be achieved.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements.  Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer should  see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly signed by proper officials before payments are made and submit the same to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for proper recording.  Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated         May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant  to see to it that   the monthly net take home pay of an employee should not be lower than  ₱5,000.00  before issuing certification to lending institutions in strict compliance to Section 48 of  Republic  Act. No. 10924 also known as General Appropriation Act  of  2018.
Henceforth,  Management should see to it that net take home pay of employees do not fall below the minimum amount set by law.

Require immediate liquidation of cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has  been served and refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been settled/liquidated in strict compliance with Section  89 of P.D. 1445 and Section  5.1.3  of  COA Circular No. 97-9002 dated February 10, 1997;

Require an official/employee who intends to be separated from the service due to reassignment/ transfer/retirement to refund un-liquidated cash advances before issuance of clearance from accountabilities

We recommended that Management  require the     OIC- Municipal Accountant to transfer and record in the Trust Fund books, Trust Liabilities-LDRRM account the unexpended balances of appropriations for LDRRMF totaling ₱5.035 million and strictly comply with Section 5.l.l0 of COA Circular No 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for proper accounting thereof.

We recommended that Management appropriate funds for MRF and immediately utilize the same for full implementation of the project so as  to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives thereof in compliance withSection 32 of   R.A. No.  9003.

We recommended that Management allocate one percent (1%) of the IRA and establish Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan in strict compliance with DILG Memo. Circular No. 2012-120 and Section 15 of RA 9344 to ensure that all plans for children are provided with funds for the implementation of programs and projects.

We recommended that Management fully implement priority PPAs of GAD and see to it  that  PPAs are in conformity with Section 1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and  NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.
We recommended that Management strictly implement and comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the segregation of solid wastes at source and establishment of a sanitary landfill.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
Require the Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.
Require the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

In view thereof, we recommended that Management submit copies of the MOA/Trust Agreement or submit justification on non-attachment of the same for the various programs/projects the  Municipality had implemented thru fund transfers to support transactions/disbursements pertaining thereto and for the evaluation and validation of these projects.
Likewise, Management should enhance the review mechanism in processing of Disbursement Vouchers. Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 3.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of  P.D. No. 1445  and  COA  Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the immediate submission of the requisite supporting documents for disbursements listed in Annex D to avoid the suspension and/or disallowance of the same and that the OIC-Municipal Accountant should ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor.
Likewise, Management should enhance  the  review  mechanism  in  processing of Disbursement   Vouchers.  Henceforth,  strictly   comply   with   Section   4(6)   of   PD No. 1445 and Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management:

In view thereof, we recommended that Management appropriate funds for MRF and immediately utilize the same for full implementation of the project so as  to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives thereof in compliance with Section 32 of   R.A. No.  9003.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management allocate one percent (1%) of the IRA and establish Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan in strict compliance with DILG Memo. Circular No. 2012-120 and Section 15 of RA 9344 to ensure that all plans for children are provided with funds for the implementation of programs and projects.

We recommended that Management fully implement priority PPAs of GAD and see to it  that  PPAs are in conformity with Section 1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and  NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA so that gender issues are addressed and women empowerment and gender equality are promoted.

We recommended that Management submit copies of the MOA/ Trust Agreement for the various programs/projects the  Municipality had implemented thru fund transfers to support transactions/disbursements pertaining thereto and for the evaluation and validation of these projects.  Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 3.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the liquidation reports with complete supporting documents relative to the disbursements for BUB. Henceforth, comply strictly with COA Circular No. 94-013 for the submission of monthly reports and supporting documents for fund transfer received from other government agencies.

Submit to the Audit Team the documentary requirements as required under  Section 3 of  COA Circular  No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review ; and


Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the aforementioned law and regulations in releasing government funds to League of Municipalities  prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor or Municipal Vice Mayor, as the case maybe.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of taxpayers and amounts due and collectible and submit the certified list to the OIC-Municipal Accountant as her basis in taking up the RPT/SET receivables and deferred income in order for the financial statements to be fairly presented and strictly comply with  Section 2.2.1  of  COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated  December 1, 2015 and Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAs for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that Management require the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit a copy of any Purchase Orders irrespective of amount, and the corresponding supporting documents to the Audit Team, within five (5) working days from perfection/issuance thereof, in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001 dated            February 12, 2009.

We recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to initiate actions for the writing-off of the subject dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Management ensure prompt and full implementation of the 20 percent development projects and programs  during the current year in order to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated as provided under Section 287 of  R.A. No. 7160 and Section 5 of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that Management require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the year ending December 31, 2016.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to Republic Act No. 10121 otherwise known “the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.

We recommended that Management fully implement the project and immediately utilize the same to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives thereof in compliance with Section 32 of R.A. No.  9003.

We recommend that Management require the OIC Municipal Accountant to initiate actions for the writing-off of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 05, 1997, particularly on the guidelines on dormant funds and pertinent provisions of 2009 Revised Rules of Procedure of the COA.

We recommended that Management submit copies of the MOA/ Trust Agreement  for the various programs/projects the  Municipality had implemented thru fund transfers to support transactions/disbursements pertaining thereto and for the evaluation and validation of these projects.  Henceforth, strictly comply with Sections 3.1 and 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the liquidation reports with complete supporting documents relative to the disbursements for BUB. Henceforth, comply strictly with COA Circular No. 94-013 for the submission of monthly reports and supporting  documents for fund transfer received from other government agencies.

We recommend that Management:
Submit to the Audit Team the documentary requirements as required under  Section 3 of  COA Circular  No. 2012-001 for evaluation and review ; and
Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the aforementioned law and regulations in releasing government funds to League of Municipalities  prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor or Municipal Vice Mayor, as the case maybe.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of taxpayers and amounts due and collectible and submit the certified list to the OIC-Municipal Accountant as her basis in taking up the RPT/SET receivables and deferred income in order for the financial statements to be fairly presented and strictly comply with  Section 2.2.1  of  COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated  December 1, 2015 and Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAs for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that Management require the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit a copy of any Purchase Orders irrespective of amount,  and the corresponding supporting documents to the Audit Team, within five (5) working days from perfection/issuance thereof, in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001 dated            February 12, 2009.

We recommended the following:
Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure the proper recording of all purchases of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period,  pursuant to Section 51 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015;
Direct the Property Officer designate to prepare and submit the SSMI to the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure fair presentation of the Office Supplies Inventory and Expense accounts in the financial statements; and
Henceforth,  require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the journal entries for all the Supplies Inventory and Supplies Expense accounts,  and prepare the corresponding adjusting entries, if warranted.

We recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to initiate actions for the writing-off of the subject dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular      No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We  recommend the following:
Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through bank;
Make a representation with any government bank for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and
Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the Management ensure prompt and full implementation of  the 20 percent development projects and programs  during the current year in order to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated as provided under Section 287 of  R.A. No. 7160 and Section 5 of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that Management require the LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the year ending  December 31, 2016.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to Republic Act No. 10121 otherwise known “the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.

We recommended that Management:
a.   Immediately form the members of the Municipality’s SWMB;
Direct the Municipal SWMB to convene regularly to enable efficient and effective performance of its functions; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 12 of R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that Management fully implement the project and immediately utilize the same to ensure the attainment of the goals and objectives thereof in compliance with Section 32 of R.A. No.  9003.
We recommended that Management strictly implement and comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the segregation of solid wastes at source and establishment of a sanitary landfill.

We recommended the following:
Cause the monitoring of GAD activities and constant reconciliation with accounting records to come up with a reliable GAD report;
Submit a copy of the corresponding  GAD Accomplishment Report to the  Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year; and
Henceforth, comply strictly with Sections  4.8 and 5.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 of DBM, NEDA, and NCRFW.

The Management ensure prompt and full implementation of the 20% development projects and programs  during the current year in order to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated as provided under Section 287 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 5 of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

The Management particularly the Local School Board to limit the utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects provided under Sections 100(c) and 272 of RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Circular (JC) No. 01s. 1998, in order to maximize the  benefits that students and teachers could avail under this fund.

The Management require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the year ending December 31, 2015.  Henceforth, comply strictly with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and closely monitor the management’s performance and compliance pursuant to RA 10121 otherwise known “the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act (PDRRMA) of 2010.

We recommended the immediate submission of Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Report of Disbursements and Payrolls, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements, as well as Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts pursuant to Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1 and see to it that these provisions should be strictly complied with.

We recommend the immediate submission of the requisite supporting documents for disbursements listed in Annex A to avoid the suspension and/or disallowance of the same. Henceforth, the OIC-Municipal Accountant should ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents, prior to the approval of the Municipal Mayor and see to it that disbursements are duly signed and approved by proper officials in strict compliance with the provisions of Sections 4(5) and 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
